plays.story: EN World's Podcast #406 livestream covers a $500 Deadfall box set, new Arcana Unleashed backgrounds, the Deathbringer RPG launch, and a Godzilla RPG tie-in — a grab-bag of premium releases and lore drops for GMs to dig into.

angles.bizzal-games: Product roundup hook: 'This Week's TTRPG Drops: A $500 Deadfall Box Set, Godzilla Stomps the Tabletop, and Deathbringer RPG Debuts' — break down what's actually in the premium box, whether it's worth the price tag, and how the new systems stack up against existing crunch.
angles.itsalreadywritten: Narrative-craft angle: 'Arcana Unleashed's New Backgrounds Are Basically Free Character Backstories — Here's How to Steal Them' — mine the new backgrounds and Deathbringer's setting lore for ready-made hooks GMs can drop straight into a campaign.

plays.story: Sly Flourish's 'Adventure Types in Rise of the Lazy Gamemaster' breaks down the structural categories of adventures GMs run, from dungeon crawls to mystery arcs.

angles.bizzal-games: Toolkit hook: 'The 5 Adventure Types Every Lazy GM Needs in Their Back Pocket' — turn Sly Flourish's framework into a practical, skimmable cheat sheet for prepping faster sessions.
angles.itsalreadywritten: Craft-analysis angle: 'What Published Adventures Get Wrong (and Right) About Structure' — use the adventure-type breakdown to critique how commercial modules are actually written and where homebrew GMs can outdo them.

plays.story: Cross-chain bridge Allbridge paused operations after a $1.65M exploit, the latest in a string of DeFi infrastructure hacks this week.

angles.itsalreadypriced: Market angle: 'Allbridge's $1.65M Hack Freezes Cross-Chain Transfers — What It Means for Tokens Stuck Mid-Bridge' — cover liquidity fallout, which assets are exposed, and how bridge risk should factor into cross-chain price plays.
angles.itsalreadywhen: Technical post-mortem angle: 'Inside the Allbridge Exploit: How Attackers Found the Gap Before Auditors Did' — walk through the attack vector and what it says about bridge security review timelines.

plays.story: A NEAR co-founder warns that AI-assisted hacking is now outpacing traditional code review processes in crypto/web3 security.

angles.itsalreadypriced: Investor-risk angle: 'AI Hackers Are Outrunning Crypto Audits, Says NEAR Co-Founder — What That Means for Your Portfolio' — translate the warning into concrete due-diligence advice for holders of audited-but-vulnerable protocols.
angles.itsalreadywhen: Security-ops angle: 'AI vs. Auditors: Why Manual Code Review Can't Keep Up With AI-Generated Exploits' — dig into the mechanics of AI-assisted vulnerability discovery and what defenders need to change.
